deep_dive.title: KVpop -- Key-Value Cache Compression with Predictive Online Pruning
deep_dive.abstract: Key-value (KV) cache growth is a major bottleneck in autoregressive decoding, as memory and bandwidth scale linearly with context length. Existing KV eviction methods often rely on static heuristics or proxy scores, which poorly track future token utility and cause brittle eviction as relevance shifts. To address this, we introduce KVpop, which learns a fixed-budget KV eviction policy by directly supervising the keep-or-drop decision. The scorer is trained against a novel future-attention target, computed efficiently without materializing dense attention maps. We further introduce a delayed memory-based scorer that, uniquely among learned eviction methods, defers scoring for a fixed number of steps to exploit near-future context. On AIME and HMMT mathematical reasoning, KVpop retains 98% of full-attention performance on Qwen3-4B at 75% KV cache compression and 97% at 88% compression, consistently outperforming established eviction baselines. Qwen3-8B shows even stronger results, reaching near-full teacher performance. These results show that supervising eviction with future-attention signals cuts memory costs while maintaining quality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: learned KV cache eviction policy with future-attention supervision
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于未来注意力监督的学习型KV缓存驱逐策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于未来注意力监督的学习型KV缓存驱逐策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于未来注意力监督的学习型KV缓存驱逐策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces KVpop, a learned KV eviction policy trained against a future-attention target, achieving superior memory efficiency on mathematical reasoning tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: KV缓存增长是自回归解码的主要瓶颈，现有驱逐方法依赖静态启发式，难以追踪未来令牌效用。本文提出KVpop，通过直接监督保留/丢弃决策学习固定预算KV驱逐策略。评分器针对新颖的未来注意力目标进行训练，无需生成密集注意力图。还引入延迟内存评分器，利用近未来上下文。在AIME和HMMT数学推理任务上，KVpop在保持精度的同时显著降低了缓存大小。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请你帮我查询有关稀疏注意力机制、KVCache压缩、高效注意力的论文, especially work that connects or combines: sparse attention mechanism in transformer models; kv cache compression techniques for large language models; efficient attention mechanisms for transformers; long context attention with sparse patterns; papers about sparse attention and kv cache compression for efficient transformers; recent advances in efficient attention mechanisms and memory compression; survey on sparse attention and key value cache optimization.

deep_dive.title: Towards Efficient Large Language Model Serving: A Survey on System-Aware KV Cache Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Despite the rapid advancements of large language models (LLMs), LLM serving systems remain memory-intensive and costly. The key-value (KV) cache, which stores KV tensors during autoregressive decoding, is crucial for enabling low-latency, high-throughput LLM inference serving. In this survey, we focus on system-aware KV infrastructure for serving LLMs (abbreviated as sKis). We revisit recent work from a system behavior perspective, organizing existing efforts into three dimensions: execution and scheduling (temporal), placement and migration (spatial), and representation and retention (structural). Furthermore, we analyze cross-behavior co-design affinity and behavior-objective links, highlighting future opportunities. Our work systematizes a rapidly evolving area, providing a foundation for understanding and innovating KV cache designs in modern LLM serving infrastructure.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: survey on system-aware KV cache optimization for efficient LLM serving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向高效LLM服务的系统感知KV缓存优化综述
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向高效LLM服务的系统感知KV缓存优化综述
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向高效LLM服务的系统感知KV缓存优化综述
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This survey provides a comprehensive overview of KV cache optimization techniques from a system perspective, categorizing methods into temporal, spatial, and structural dimensions, and highlighting future research directions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型推理服务中KV缓存是内存消耗和延迟的关键瓶颈。现有工作分散且缺乏系统视角。本文从系统行为角度综述KV缓存优化，将其分为执行调度（时间）、放置迁移（空间）和表示保留（结构）三个维度，并分析了跨行为协同设计和行为-目标关联。该综述系统梳理了当前方法，识别了未来机遇，为构建高效LLM服务系统提供了重要参考。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 大语言模型推理服务中KV缓存是内存消耗和延迟的关键瓶颈。现有工作分散且缺乏系统视角。本文从系统行为角度综述KV缓存优化，将其分为执行调度（时间）、放置迁移（空间）和表示保留（结构）三个维度，并分析了跨行为协同设计和行为-目标关联。该综述系统梳理了当前方法，识别了未来机遇，为构建高效LLM服务系统提供了重要参考。

deep_dive.title: Scene Graph Thinking: Reinforcing Structured Visual Reasoning for Multimodal Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated strong perception and reasoning capabilities. However, most existing models focus on isolated objects and neglect structured relationships for efficient target navigation, limiting their performance on visually intensive tasks. To address this challenge, we introduce Scene Graph Thinking (SaGe), a novel paradigm that enables fine-grained and structured visual reasoning through explicit scene-graph representations. Specifically, we first introduce an automated data engine that converts flat image-text corpora into structured scene graphs, where hierarchical entities constitute the nodes and diverse visual relations define the edges. Building upon this, we construct 120K high-quality training data by sampling reasoning traces from scene graphs. Then, two-stage graph-aligned post-training paradigms are introduced, where supervised fine-tuning internalizes MLLMs with structured reasoning, and subsequent reinforcement fine-tuning proposes node-as-proxy graph rewards to consolidate efficient graph exploration. With curated data and graph-aligned training, our approach achieves significant improvements across eight multimodal benchmarks, demonstrating strong effectiveness on fine-grained perception and reasoning tasks. Code is available at https://github.com/zwyang6/SaGe.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: structured visual reasoning via scene graphs for MLLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过场景图进行结构化视觉推理以改进MLLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过场景图进行结构化视觉推理以改进MLLM
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过场景图进行结构化视觉推理以改进MLLM
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SaGe enhances MLLM visual reasoning by introducing explicit scene-graph representations and an automated data engine for graph construction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大语言模型在视觉推理任务中常忽略物体间的结构化关系，限制了对复杂场景的理解。本文提出场景图思维（SaGe）范式，通过自动数据引擎将图像-文本对转换为结构化场景图，其中层次化实体为节点、视觉关系为边。在多个视觉推理基准上，SaGe显著提升MLLM的细粒度推理能力。该方法为结构化多模态推理提供了有效框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多模态大语言模型在视觉推理任务中常忽略物体间的结构化关系，限制了对复杂场景的理解。本文提出场景图思维（SaGe）范式，通过自动数据引擎将图像-文本对转换为结构化场景图，其中层次化实体为节点、视觉关系为边。在多个视觉推理基准上，SaGe显著提升MLLM的细粒度推理能力。该方法为结构化多模态推理提供了有效框架。

deep_dive.title: CAIRN: Cross-Room 3D Scene Understanding with Topology-Aware Large Multimodal Models
deep_dive.abstract: Existing 3D scene-grounded Large Language Models (3D-LLMs) focus on answering questions grounded in simplified single-room 3D scenes, lacking the ability to reason over real-world household environments containing multiple interconnected rooms and diverse object categories. We introduce CAIRN, a topology-aware 3D-LLM for multi-room 3D scene understanding. CAIRN aligns transformer attention with scene hierarchy, giving the model explicit awareness of object-level relations and room-level connectivity. It enriches object tokens with room-local relational context via a graph neural network, introduces learned room tokens for room-level abstraction, and applies a hierarchical attention mask with geometric bias to route information according to scene topology. CAIRN is developed on CAIRN-MR, a benchmark we introduce on HM3D for multi-room 3D scene understanding, covering grounding, captioning, and four question-answering tasks that progressively evaluate from intra-room perception to cross-room reasoning. Experiments show that CAIRN outperforms prior 3D-LLMs by a large margin across all CAIRN-MR tasks while remaining competitive on five single-room benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 拓扑感知的3D大语言模型用于跨房间场景理解
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 拓扑感知的3D大语言模型用于跨房间场景理解
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 拓扑感知的3D大语言模型用于跨房间场景理解
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CAIRN, a topology-aware 3D-LLM for multi-room 3D scene understanding. Uses GNN for relational context, room tokens for abstraction, and hierarchical attention mask. Enables reasoning over interconnected rooms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有3D-LLM局限于简化单房间场景，无法处理真实多房间环境。本文提出CAIRN，一种拓扑感知的3D-LLM，通过图神经网络注入物体关系、引入房间令牌实现层次抽象，并采用层次化注意力掩码引导信息路由。在MultiRoom3D数据集上，CAIRN在跨房间问答任务中显著优于先前方法，实现了对复杂家居环境的深度理解。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有3D-LLM局限于简化单房间场景，无法处理真实多房间环境。本文提出CAIRN，一种拓扑感知的3D-LLM，通过图神经网络注入物体关系、引入房间令牌实现层次抽象，并采用层次化注意力掩码引导信息路由。在MultiRoom3D数据集上，CAIRN在跨房间问答任务中显著优于先前方法，实现了对复杂家居环境的深度理解。

deep_dive.title: TriRoute: Unified Learned Routing for Joint Adaptive Attention, Experts, and KV-Cache Allocation
deep_dive.abstract: Conditional computation can decouple language model quality from per-token inference cost, yet leading techniques act on a single axis in isolation: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) sparsifies the FFN, Mixture-of-Depths (MoD) skips whole transformer blocks, and KV-cache quantization compresses attention memory. We argue these three decisions (attention resolution, expert selection, and cache bit-width) are strongly coupled and should be made jointly: a token rare enough to warrant full attention may also need high-precision caching regardless of which expert processes it. We introduce TriRoute, a single lightweight controller shared across all three axes that, for every token at every layer, emits a coordinated policy: (i) an attention mode (skip/local/full), (ii) a sparse set of FFN experts (with a null expert recovering MoD), and (iii) a KV-cache bit-width. The controller trains end-to-end via a heterogeneous relaxation (Gumbel-Softmax with straight-through estimation for categorical decisions and load-balanced top-k gating for experts) under a Lagrangian budget constraint that turns the average compute and memory cost into a controllable knob. We identify a cross-axis routing-collapse cascade in naive joint training, where collapse on one axis propagates to the others, and address it with per-axis normalization and a coupling-aware balancing loss. On decoder-only models from 160M to 1.3B parameters at compute-optimal token counts, TriRoute Pareto-dominates the best independent MoD+MoE+KV-quantization combination at matched inference FLOPs and memory, while better preserving tail-case robustness on rare entities, code, and arithmetic that pure perplexity optimization erodes. Post-hoc analysis reveals interpretable structure: the controller allocates full attention and high-precision cache to sentence-initial positions, rare subwords, and named entities, while cheaply routing function words.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: unified learning for KV-cache allocation and attention sparsity
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联合学习KV缓存分配与注意力稀疏化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联合学习KV缓存分配与注意力稀疏化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联合学习KV缓存分配与注意力稀疏化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TriRoute jointly optimizes attention resolution, expert selection, and KV-cache bit-width via a lightweight controller, reducing inference cost while maintaining quality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有条件计算方法（MoE、MoD、KV缓存量化）各自独立优化不同维度，忽略了它们之间的耦合关系。本文提出TriRoute，一个轻量级联合路由控制器，为每个层的每个令牌协调决策注意力分辨率、专家选择和缓存位宽。实验表明，TriRoute在保持模型质量的同时显著降低推理成本。该方法为高效Transformer推理提供了统一的优化视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有条件计算方法（MoE、MoD、KV缓存量化）各自独立优化不同维度，忽略了它们之间的耦合关系。本文提出TriRoute，一个轻量级联合路由控制器，为每个层的每个令牌协调决策注意力分辨率、专家选择和缓存位宽。实验表明，TriRoute在保持模型质量的同时显著降低推理成本。该方法为高效Transformer推理提供了统一的优化视角。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: kv cache compression techniques for large language models

deep_dive.title: Uncertainty-gated selection for block-sparse attention
deep_dive.abstract: Block-sparse attention scales long-context language models by replacing the O(N^2) softmax with a per-query top-k selection over key blocks. This cutoff is myopic: when the k-th and (k+1)-th blocks are nearly tied in score, the selector commits without spending extra budget, and a dropped block carrying answer evidence is unrecoverable downstream. We propose a value-of-information router that measures, for each query, how decisively the top-k cut was made, and doubles the kept set for the queries where that gap is smallest; the rule is backbone-agnostic and stacks with existing block-scoring methods such as Quest. On LongBench-v2 medium at n=215 (the entire dataset subset), router-on-Quest reaches paired recall 0.75 vs. top-k 0.47 -- +28 pp over the SSA-style baseline (McNemar p<0.01) -- and lands within 2 pp of dense on RULER NIAH multikey at the same context. The lift reproduces on four models from three architectures (Qwen2.5, Mistral-Nemo, Qwen3.6). At 128K, the router preserves 0.81 and 0.89 of dense accuracy on Qwen2.5-7B-1M and Qwen3.6 (vs. SSA-style top-k at 0.09 on the former) while the fused selection-plus-kernel pipeline runs at 0.62x and 0.80x dense wall time.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uncertainty-gated selection for block-sparse attention in long-context LMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 长上下文语言模型中的不确定性门控块稀疏注意力选择
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 长上下文语言模型中的不确定性门控块稀疏注意力选择
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 长上下文语言模型中的不确定性门控块稀疏注意力选择
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a value-of-information router that adaptively doubles kept blocks for queries with low decisiveness in block-sparse attention, improving recall by 28pp on LongBench-v2.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 块稀疏注意力通过每查询的top-k块选择降低复杂度，但当第k块和第k+1块分数接近时，丢弃的块可能包含证据且无法恢复。本文提出信息价值路由器，衡量每个查询的top-k截断决策决定性，对差距最小的查询加倍保留块数。该路由器与现有块评分方法（如Quest）结合，在LongBench-v2上召回率从0.47提升至0.75，提升28个百分点。

deep_dive.title: Linear Attention Architectures: Mechanisms, Trade-offs, and Cross-Layer Routing
deep_dive.abstract: Self-attention lets each token retrieve information from the full context, but its quadratic cost in sequence length limits training and inference at long context. This paper presents a comparative study of softmax attention and four recent recurrent linear-attention architectures: DeltaNet, Gated DeltaNet, Kimi Delta Attention, and Gated DeltaNet-2. We express these mechanisms in a common recurrent-memory notation, making explicit how they differ in expressivity, memory decay, erase and write control, training throughput, and implementation complexity. Our experiments center on 350M-parameter models trained for 15B tokens, and include optimizer and learning-rate comparisons, hybrid-versus-pure stack comparisons, sequence-length runtime measurements, larger DeltaNet runs at 1.3B and 3B parameters, and a small set of downstream evaluations. The reported speed results measure training throughput and iteration time; we do not provide an empirical inference-speed benchmark. Within the reported 350M-parameter, 15B-token sweep, Kimi Delta Attention with Muon reaches the lowest final validation loss, a pure Gated DeltaNet stack trained with AdamW has the highest normalized training throughput, hybrid stacks generally improve loss at a throughput cost, and Muon consistently lowers final validation loss relative to AdamW in the matched architecture settings we evaluate. We introduce and evaluate lightweight cross-layer routing mechanisms for DeltaNet-style memories. The most natural DeltaNet-inspired formulation, forwarding a lower layer's delta-rule write error into the next layer's value target, does not improve over matched baselines. Routing into the aligned hidden stream and forwarding the write value instead yields a modest improvement in the matched runs we report: Cross-Layer Value Routing (CLVR) lowers final validation loss for both DeltaNet and Gated DeltaNet.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: comparative study of linear attention architectures for efficient transformers
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 线性注意力架构比较研究，实现高效Transformer
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 线性注意力架构比较研究，实现高效Transformer
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 线性注意力架构比较研究，实现高效Transformer
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper compares softmax attention and four linear-attention variants, analyzing expressivity, memory, and throughput, with experiments on 350M-parameter models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 标准自注意力的二次复杂度限制了长序列训练和推理。本文对软注意力与四种近期循环线性注意力架构（DeltaNet、门控DeltaNet、Kimi Delta注意力、门控DeltaNet-2）进行了系统比较。通过统一的循环记忆表示，揭示了它们在表达能力、记忆衰减和训练吞吐量上的差异。300M参数模型实验表明，混合线性注意力架构在长上下文任务中实现了效率与质量的平衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 标准自注意力的二次复杂度限制了长序列训练和推理。本文对软注意力与四种近期循环线性注意力架构（DeltaNet、门控DeltaNet、Kimi Delta注意力、门控DeltaNet-2）进行了系统比较。通过统一的循环记忆表示，揭示了它们在表达能力、记忆衰减和训练吞吐量上的差异。300M参数模型实验表明，混合线性注意力架构在长上下文任务中实现了效率与质量的平衡。

deep_dive.title: What to Keep, What to Forget: A Rate--Distortion View of Memory Compaction in LLMs and Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large language models, and the agents built on them, spend an ever-growing share of their compute and memory on remembering: caching attention keys and values, carrying long prompts, maintaining recurrent state, and storing what happened in previous turns and sessions. Because none of this memory is free, four largely separate research communities have each learned to compact it. They evict or quantize the KV cache, prune or distill prompts, bound architectural state, and consolidate agent memory. We argue that these are instances of one problem: a rate--distortion decision about what context-derived information to retain versus discard, at what fidelity, under a resource budget, so as to preserve downstream task utility. We make this lens precise with a single compaction objective and a layer-agnostic lower bound, use it to build a seven-axis taxonomy that classifies methods from across the stack uniformly, and use it to transfer mechanisms between layers that have never been connected, from serving-stack KV management to agent long-term memory. Two patterns hold across the survey. At every layer the signal that decides what to keep is attention magnitude or recency, and it fails in the same way everywhere, by discarding, before the query is known and with no way to undo it, information the query later needs. And while compression is measured carefully on single-turn long context, the repeated compaction that agents actually perform is almost never measured, and no benchmark holds one budget axis across all the layers at once. We turn both observations into a benchmark proposal, a small reference experiment, and a set of compaction-aware design principles, and we map the open problems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: survey on memory compaction including KV cache compression
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 包括KV缓存压缩的内存压缩综述
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 包括KV缓存压缩的内存压缩综述
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 包括KV缓存压缩的内存压缩综述
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Unifies KV cache eviction, prompt pruning, state bounding, and agent memory compaction under a rate-distortion framework, surveying methods and providing theoretical perspective.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 将LLM和智能体中的KV缓存淘汰、提示压缩、状态边界化、智能体记忆压缩统一为一个率-失真决策问题：在资源预算下保留或丢弃上下文信息。系统综述了各类方法的形式化表示和权衡，并提出未来方向。该视角有助于厘清内存压缩研究的底层原理。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 将LLM和智能体中的KV缓存淘汰、提示压缩、状态边界化、智能体记忆压缩统一为一个率-失真决策问题：在资源预算下保留或丢弃上下文信息。系统综述了各类方法的形式化表示和权衡，并提出未来方向。该视角有助于厘清内存压缩研究的底层原理。

deep_dive.title: Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent for Multimodal Understanding, Generation, and Editing
deep_dive.abstract: Recent unified multimodal models show a single architecture can jointly perform vision/language understanding and image generation/editing. However, they repeatedly feed all historical visual and textual inputs into a shared context window, limiting long-horizon multimodal dialogue due to visual token explosion and unreliable cross-turn referencing. We propose a Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent that externalizes visual information into an Episodic Visual Memory and selectively reactivates relevant episodes during reasoning. The agent consists of a Perceptual Abstraction Engine for structured visual abstraction, a Cognitive Retrieval Engine for cross-turn memory retrieval, and a Multimodal Executive Controller for autonomous task inference and action planning. To address the lack of turn-level retrieval supervision in existing datasets, we develop a Unified Scenario Engine that programmatically generates structured multi-turn conversations with fine-grained retrieval annotations, enabling reinforcement learning to optimize abstraction and retrieval policies. We also construct a long-horizon visual-dialogue benchmark stratified by difficulty to evaluate episodic visual recall. Our 8B agent achieves 91.4% retrieval accuracy over 20-turn sessions, surpassing 32B baselines by +8.2% while nearly halving per-turn inference time (23.1s -> 12.7s). We further present the Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent Harness (CMA-Harness), a tool-augmented deployment of the same cognitive structure integrating persistent multimodal memory, web access, image generation/editing/composition tools, and OpenAI-compatible serving. Structured memory and modular decision-making offer a more scalable, efficient paradigm for long-horizon multimodal agents than monolithic parameter scaling. Code: https://github.com/caseclose/cma-harness ; Project page: https://caseclose.github.io/cma-harness/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multimodal agent with episodic visual memory for long-horizon dialogue
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态智能体与情景视觉记忆实现长程对话
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多模态智能体与情景视觉记忆实现长程对话
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多模态智能体与情景视觉记忆实现长程对话
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent that externalizes visual information into episodic memory, enabling long-horizon multimodal dialogue by selective reactivation of relevant episodes, solving visual token explosion and cross-turn referencing issues.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有统一多模态模型将所有历史视觉和文本输入重复送入共享上下文窗口，导致视觉令牌爆炸和跨轮引用不可靠，限制了长程对话。本文提出认知结构多模态智能体，将视觉信息外部化到情景视觉记忆中，并在推理时选择性地重新激活相关情景。该智能体包含感知抽象引擎、认知检索引擎和多模态执行控制器，有效解决了视觉记忆问题，提升了长程多模态对话能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有统一多模态模型将所有历史视觉和文本输入重复送入共享上下文窗口，导致视觉令牌爆炸和跨轮引用不可靠，限制了长程对话。本文提出认知结构多模态智能体，将视觉信息外部化到情景视觉记忆中，并在推理时选择性地重新激活相关情景。该智能体包含感知抽象引擎、认知检索引擎和多模态执行控制器，有效解决了视觉记忆问题，提升了长程多模态对话能力。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态大模型相关论文，包括理解、检索、幻觉等方向, especially work that connects or combines: multimodal understanding models; multimodal hallucination detection; multimodal reasoning tasks; papers on multimodal understanding in large language models; causes and mitigation of hallucination in multimodal models; architecture of large multimodal models combining vision and language.

quick_skim.title: Q-TriM: Question-Guided Tri-Modal Attention for Audio-Visual Question Answering
quick_skim.abstract: Audio-Visual Question Answering (AVQA) extends classical VQA by requiring joint reasoning over video and synchronized audio. However, many AVQA systems rely on deeply stacked layers of self- and cross attention across text, video, and audio. Such sequential stacking may incur loss of information such as subtle inter-modal cues over the layers, causing errors to accumulate across sequential attention layers during the fusion. We introduce Q-TriM which performs multi-modal fusion in a shallow and parallel manner instead of a deep and sequential manner. For Q-TriM, we propose a novel framework for attention operation incorporating video and audio conditioned on text. As a result, we obtain not only standard cross attention outputs but also Tri-Modal Attention representations in which Query, Key, and Value come from distinct modalities. These attention representations are combined in parallel at a single stage, thus avoiding the multi-modal fusion with deep stacks in order to mitigate error accumulation and depth-induced issues. Q-TriM achieves state-of-the-art performance on three AVQA benchmarks, including substantial gains on MUSIC-AVQA-R, which demonstrates its robustness and out-of-distribution generalization. Code is available at https://github.com/Sunghun95/Q-TriM
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: question-guided tri-modal attention for audio-visual question answering
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 问题引导的三模态注意力用于音频-视觉问答
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 问题引导的三模态注意力用于音频-视觉问答
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 问题引导的三模态注意力用于音频-视觉问答
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Q-TriM proposes a shallow parallel tri-modal attention mechanism that fuses text, video, and audio effectively, improving performance on audio-visual question answering by reducing error accumulation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 音频-视觉问答需联合推理视频和音频，现有系统采用深度堆叠自注意力，导致跨模态信息随层数累积丢失。本文提出Q-TriM，以浅层并行方式执行三模态融合，引入问题引导的视频和音频条件注意力。该框架同时生成标准交叉注意力输出和三模态交互特征，显著减少了信息损失。在多个AVQA基准上，该方法以更少参数取得了更优结果，验证了并行融合的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 音频-视觉问答需联合推理视频和音频，现有系统采用深度堆叠自注意力，导致跨模态信息随层数累积丢失。本文提出Q-TriM，以浅层并行方式执行三模态融合，引入问题引导的视频和音频条件注意力。该框架同时生成标准交叉注意力输出和三模态交互特征，显著减少了信息损失。在多个AVQA基准上，该方法以更少参数取得了更优结果，验证了并行融合的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Transferability Between Understanding and Generation in Unified Multimodal Models
quick_skim.abstract: Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) integrate image understanding and generation within a single architecture, yet how the two tasks interact remains understudied. We investigate $\boldsymbol{\mathsf{transferability}}$ in UMMs: whether training a capability on one task improves the same capability on the other without explicit supervision. Through controlled experiments, we empirically find that transferability depends on architecture-models with fully shared transformer backbone and a unified visual encoder exhibit consistent cross-task transfer, while loosely coupled designs show little or none. Leveraging this transferability, we propose a practical training strategy. The most straightforward way to improve a target generative capability (e.g., counting) is to fine-tune generation directly, but this can degrade visual quality due to distribution shift. Instead, we train the corresponding understanding task and let it transfer into generation, which improves capability-specific generative performance while minimizing distribution shift. We validate this across three capabilities-counting, spatial relation, and text recognition/generation-showing that cross-task transferability can be systematically exploited in UMMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transferability between understanding and generation in unified multimodal models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成之间的可迁移性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成之间的可迁移性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成之间的可迁移性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies transferability in UMMs: training a capability on one task improves the same capability on another. Shows full-sharing architectures enable cross-task transfer and proposes a practical training strategy leveraging this.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 统一多模态模型(UMM)同时处理图像理解和生成，但两者如何相互影响未知。本文通过控制实验发现，架构设计关键：全共享Transformer主干与统一视觉编码器能产生跨任务迁移，而松散耦合则无。基于此提出训练策略，在无监督情况下利用理解能力提升生成质量，反之亦然。在多项基准上验证了迁移的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 统一多模态模型(UMM)同时处理图像理解和生成，但两者如何相互影响未知。本文通过控制实验发现，架构设计关键：全共享Transformer主干与统一视觉编码器能产生跨任务迁移，而松散耦合则无。基于此提出训练策略，在无监督情况下利用理解能力提升生成质量，反之亦然。在多项基准上验证了迁移的有效性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态大模型相关论文，包括理解、检索、幻觉等方向, especially work that connects or combines: multimodal understanding models; multimodal hallucination detection; multimodal reasoning tasks; papers on multimodal understanding in large language models; causes and mitigation of hallucination in multimodal models; architecture of large multimodal models combining vision and language.

quick_skim.title: $C^3$ASD: Multi-Level Consistency-Driven Representation Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Active Speaker Detection determines whether a visible person in a video is speaking at each moment. While recent audio-visual fusion methods perform well on clean data, they degrade under real-world corruptions such as background noise, occlusion, or simultaneous modality degradation. We attribute this limitation to the absence of explicit consistency constraints that promote robust, semantically aligned representations across modalities. Without such guidance, models tend to learn fragile modality-specific shortcuts that fail under corrupted conditions. We propose $C^3$ASD, a multi-level consistency-driven framework with three complementary constraints: embedding-level inter-modality consistency aligns audio-visual representations during speech; sequence-level intra-modality consistency separates speaking and non-speaking clusters via track-aware contrastive learning; and prediction-level consistency stabilizes fusion through knowledge distillation. Extensive experiments demonstrate significant improvements under diverse audio, visual and joint corruptions, while maintaining competitive performance on clean data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-level consistency for robust audio-visual representation learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多级一致性约束的音频-视觉表示学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多级一致性约束的音频-视觉表示学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多级一致性约束的音频-视觉表示学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces C3ASD, a multi-level consistency framework with embedding-level, sequence-level, and modality-level constraints for robust active speaker detection under corruptions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现实世界噪声、遮挡等干扰下多模态融合性能下降的问题，提出C3ASD框架，通过嵌入级、序列级和模态级三级一致性约束，促进音频-视觉表征的对齐与鲁棒性。实验证明在多种损坏条件下显著优于现有方法，推动了多模态理解的鲁棒性研究。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现实世界噪声、遮挡等干扰下多模态融合性能下降的问题，提出C3ASD框架，通过嵌入级、序列级和模态级三级一致性约束，促进音频-视觉表征的对齐与鲁棒性。实验证明在多种损坏条件下显著优于现有方法，推动了多模态理解的鲁棒性研究。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Scene Priors: Fine-Grained Traffic Scene Reasoning with Benchmarking and Query-Guided Small-Object Focus
quick_skim.abstract: In safety-critical traffic scenarios, answering complex questions relies on minute, localized visual cues. However, standard Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) tend to over-attend to backgrounds, overwhelming crucial small objects during visual-language alignment, a failure mode we term 'critical evidence dilution.' Furthermore, existing visual question answering (VQA) datasets rarely expose this flaw, as they lack large-scale, distractor-heavy evaluations that require pinpointing local evidence. To bridge this evaluation and architecture gap, we introduce the Fine-Grained Traffic Reasoning Benchmark (FGTR-Bench) and the Text-Guided Small-Object Reasoning MLLM (TSR-MLLM). FGTR-Bench comprises 40,236 single-image Multiple-Choice Questions (MCQs) created via multi-agent generation, consistency checks, and expert audits, alongside a disjoint 4,947-sample blind test split. To resolve evidence dilution, TSR-MLLM, built on Qwen3-VL-4B, uses a query-conditioned Text-Guided Small-Object Focus (TG-SOF) map. Applied once at the decoder boundary, the map adds sparse Top-K gated residuals to the most question-relevant vision slots while leaving text tokens unchanged. Together with lightweight decoder adaptation, TSR-MLLM preserves single-pass inference without external detectors or image re-encoding. Under matched settings, TSR-MLLM outperforms the strongest 4B baseline by 2.1 points on FGTR-Bench (74.1% overall), with larger gains on evidence-local tracks. Furthermore, it remains competitive on DriveQA-V (CARLA Signs) under greedy decoding without task-specific fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: fine-grained traffic scene multimodal reasoning with benchmark and small-object focus
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 细粒度交通场景多模态推理，含基准和小目标聚焦
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 细粒度交通场景多模态推理，含基准和小目标聚焦
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 细粒度交通场景多模态推理，含基准和小目标聚焦
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work addresses the tendency of MLLMs to overlook small objects in traffic scenes by introducing a benchmark and a query-guided small-object reasoning model that improves fine-grained multimodal reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大语言模型在交通场景中倾向于过度关注背景而忽略小目标，导致推理错误。本文构建了细粒度交通推理基准，包含大量干扰项评估；并提出文本引导的小目标推理模型，通过查询引导的注意力聚焦于关键局部区域。该模型在基准上显著提升了复杂交通问题回答的准确性，揭示了当前模型在局部化推理中的不足。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态大语言模型在交通场景中倾向于过度关注背景而忽略小目标，导致推理错误。本文构建了细粒度交通推理基准，包含大量干扰项评估；并提出文本引导的小目标推理模型，通过查询引导的注意力聚焦于关键局部区域。该模型在基准上显著提升了复杂交通问题回答的准确性，揭示了当前模型在局部化推理中的不足。

quick_skim.title: AnchorPrune: Relevance-Anchored Contextual Expansion for Visual Token Pruning
quick_skim.abstract: Large vision-language models incur substantial inference costs because high-resolution inputs introduce thousands of visual tokens, many of which are redundant for a given query. Existing pruning methods often combine query relevance and token diversity, yet these objectives can conflict under aggressive compression: relevance-driven selection may overconcentrate the budget on correlated local evidence, while diversity-driven selection may suppress indispensable tokens or retain distinct but uninformative regions. We introduce AnchorPrune, a training-free framework that first constructs a protected relevance anchor and then expands it with complementary visual context. AnchorPrune adaptively determines the anchor size from the novelty profile of relevance-ranked tokens, preserving a compact set of query-critical evidence, and allocates the remaining budget through importance-weighted novelty to recover informative, non-redundant context relative to the anchor. This ordered design prevents contextual expansion from displacing indispensable query cues while improving overall visual coverage. AnchorPrune is lightweight, architecture-aware, and requires neither retraining nor model modification. Across image and video vision-language models and benchmarks, it consistently improves the accuracy-efficiency trade-off over training-free baselines, particularly under severe compression. On LLaVA-NeXT-7B, AnchorPrune preserves 97.6% of full-token performance using only 160 of 2,880 visual tokens. These results establish relevance-anchored contextual expansion as an effective principle for efficient multimodal inference. Code is available at https://github.com/MULTI-cau/AnchorPrune.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: visual token pruning for efficient vision-language models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉token剪枝用于高效视觉语言模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉token剪枝用于高效视觉语言模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉token剪枝用于高效视觉语言模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AnchorPrune introduces a training-free visual token pruning framework that constructs a relevance anchor and expands with complementary context, reducing inference cost in VLMs without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大视觉语言模型推理成本高昂的问题，AnchorPrune提出了一种免训练的视觉token剪枝框架。它首先构建一个受保护的相关性锚点，然后自适应地扩展互补上下文，避免剪枝时相关性与多样性目标的冲突。在保持多模态理解性能的同时，显著减少了视觉token数量，从而降低计算和内存开销。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大视觉语言模型推理成本高昂的问题，AnchorPrune提出了一种免训练的视觉token剪枝框架。它首先构建一个受保护的相关性锚点，然后自适应地扩展互补上下文，避免剪枝时相关性与多样性目标的冲突。在保持多模态理解性能的同时，显著减少了视觉token数量，从而降低计算和内存开销。

quick_skim.abstract: Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) integrate image understanding and generation within a single architecture, yet how the two tasks interact remains understudied. We investigate $\boldsymbol{\mathsf{transferability}}$ in UMMs: whether training a capability on one task improves the same capability on the other without explicit supervision. Through controlled experiments, we empirically find that transferability depends on architecture-models with fully shared transformer backbone and a unified visual encoder exhibit consistent cross-task transfer, while loosely coupled designs show little or none. Leveraging this transferability, we propose a practical training strategy. The most straightforward way to improve a target generative capability (e.g., counting) is to fine-tune generation directly, but this can degrade visual quality due to distribution shift. Instead, we train the corresponding understanding task and let it transfer into generation, which improves capability-specific generative performance while minimizing distribution shift. quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: investigates transferability in unified multimodal models with shared backbone
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究统一多模态模型中共享主干网络的迁移性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究统一多模态模型中共享主干网络的迁移性

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Unified multimodal models with fully shared transformer and unified visual encoder enable cross-task transfer between understanding and generation, while loosely coupled designs show little transfer.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了统一多模态模型中理解与生成任务之间的迁移性，发现完全共享Transformer主干和统一视觉编码器的架构能够实现跨任务的无监督迁移，而松耦合设计则几乎没有迁移效果。基于此，作者提出了一种利用迁移性提升目标生成能力的训练策略。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: architecture of large multimodal models combining vision and language

quick_skim.title: Seeing and Reflecting: Multimodal Memory-Enhanced Agent Collaboration for Recommendation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agentic recommender systems show promise in modeling user preferences through natural-language reasoning, yet they remain limited by text-centric inputs and coarse-grained memory updates, making agents prone to missing visual evidence, semantic noise, and preference drift. To address these limitations, we propose MMEACR, a Multimodal Memory-Enhanced Agent Collaboration framework for recommendation. MMEACR introduces a dual-track memory architecture that separates interpretable agent reasoning from fine-grained multimodal matching. In the reasoning track, collaborative User and Item Memory Agents maintain persistent multimodal memories and update them through an attribute-guided reinforcement-and-reflection mechanism. In the matching track, a decoupled multi-modal embedding memory is built from raw interaction narratives and item images to preserve detailed cross-modal signals beyond structured memory updates. The two tracks are integrated through weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion to produce robust and interpretable rankings. Experiments on three real-world domains show that MMEACR achieves strong overall performance against competitive LLM-based and agent-based baselines, with notable gains in visually grounded recommendation scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal memory-enhanced agent collaboration for recommendation with dual-track memory architecture
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于推荐的多模态记忆增强代理协作，具有双轨记忆架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于推荐的多模态记忆增强代理协作，具有双轨记忆架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于推荐的多模态记忆增强代理协作，具有双轨记忆架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MMEACR uses dual-track memory with user/item agents to integrate multimodal evidence for recommendation, improving preference modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于LLM的推荐系统受限于文本输入和粗粒度记忆。本文提出多模态记忆增强代理协作框架MMEACR，引入双轨记忆架构，分离可解释推理与细粒度多模态匹配，通过属性引导的强化和反思更新多模态记忆。实验证明其在捕获多模态偏好方面的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 基于LLM的推荐系统受限于文本输入和粗粒度记忆。本文提出多模态记忆增强代理协作框架MMEACR，引入双轨记忆架构，分离可解释推理与细粒度多模态匹配，通过属性引导的强化和反思更新多模态记忆。实验证明其在捕获多模态偏好方面的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Scene Graph Thinking: Reinforcing Structured Visual Reasoning for Multimodal Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated strong perception and reasoning capabilities. However, most existing models focus on isolated objects and neglect structured relationships for efficient target navigation, limiting their performance on visually intensive tasks. To address this challenge, we introduce Scene Graph Thinking (SaGe), a novel paradigm that enables fine-grained and structured visual reasoning through explicit scene-graph representations. Specifically, we first introduce an automated data engine that converts flat image-text corpora into structured scene graphs, where hierarchical entities constitute the nodes and diverse visual relations define the edges. Building upon this, we construct 120K high-quality training data by sampling reasoning traces from scene graphs. Then, two-stage graph-aligned post-training paradigms are introduced, where supervised fine-tuning internalizes MLLMs with structured reasoning, and subsequent reinforcement fine-tuning proposes node-as-proxy graph rewards to consolidate efficient graph exploration. With curated data and graph-aligned training, our approach achieves significant improvements across eight multimodal benchmarks, demonstrating strong effectiveness on fine-grained perception and reasoning tasks. Code is available at https://github.com/zwyang6/SaGe.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured visual reasoning via scene graph in MLLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过场景图实现MLLM中的结构化视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过场景图实现MLLM中的结构化视觉推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过场景图实现MLLM中的结构化视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Scene Graph Thinking (SaGe), a paradigm that converts flat image-text data into scene graphs to enable fine-grained structured visual reasoning in MLLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大模型仅关注孤立对象而忽视结构化关系的问题，提出SaGe范式：通过自动化数据引擎将图像-文本语料转换为场景图，其中层次化实体为节点、视觉关系为边。基于此构建12个训练集，使MLLM学会显式利用结构化信息，在视觉推理任务上显著超越基线。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多模态大模型仅关注孤立对象而忽视结构化关系的问题，提出SaGe范式：通过自动化数据引擎将图像-文本语料转换为场景图，其中层次化实体为节点、视觉关系为边。基于此构建12个训练集，使MLLM学会显式利用结构化信息，在视觉推理任务上显著超越基线。

quick_skim.title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) have recently achieved substantial progress in natural language generation tasks. Recent research demonstrates that adaptive token generation ordering can significantly improve performance in mathematical reasoning and code synthesis applications. In this work, we investigate the optimization of generation order for both text-to-image synthesis and multimodal understanding. We first establish that, unlike structured problems in language generation such as Sudoku puzzles, model logits alone are insufficient for determining optimal generation sequences in text-to-image generation and multimodal understanding. To address this challenge, we introduce a learnable control module trained via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to determine the generation order. Our results demonstrate that learning this control block substantially improves both text-to-image alignment and multimodal understanding in DLMs. In particular, it enhances the model's ability to capture fine-grained spatial relationships in generated images while also strengthening performance on multimodal reasoning and comprehension tasks. We evaluate our framework on GenEval, an object-focused benchmark for text-to-image alignment, where it achieves 4.08% relative improvements. In addition, experiments on VLMEvalKit confirm 4.85% relative improvements in multimodal understanding, highlighting the broad effectiveness of our approach.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimizing generation order for multimodal masked diffusion models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 优化多模态掩码扩散模型的生成顺序
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 优化多模态掩码扩散模型的生成顺序
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 优化多模态掩码扩散模型的生成顺序
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work optimizes generation order in multimodal diffusion models using reinforcement learning, improving text-to-image and multimodal understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散语言模型在文本生成中已取得进展，自适应生成顺序能提升性能。本文将其扩展到文本到图像生成和多模态理解。发现模型logits不足以确定最优生成顺序，因此引入可学习的控制模块，通过群组相对策略优化（GRPO）训练。实验表明，优化的生成顺序在图像生成质量和多模态理解任务上均有提升。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散语言模型在文本生成中已取得进展，自适应生成顺序能提升性能。本文将其扩展到文本到图像生成和多模态理解。发现模型logits不足以确定最优生成顺序，因此引入可学习的控制模块，通过群组相对策略优化（GRPO）训练。实验表明，优化的生成顺序在图像生成质量和多模态理解任务上均有提升。

quick_skim.title: Segmentation before Answering: Pixel Grounding for MLLM Visual Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advancements in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have evolved from static perception to interleaved visual-language reasoning, often referred to as ``thinking with images''. A basic operation in this reasoning process is to zoom in on regions of interest (often represented with bounding boxes) to acquire finer visual details. In this paper, we propose \textbf{Seg}mentation before \textbf{Answer}ing (SegAnswer), which shifts the unit of zoom-in from the popular bounding box to pixel-level segmentation mask. By employing fine-grained masks to isolate the target area from cluttered environments, segmented visual input yields a more precise region of interest, effectively filtering out redundant background and interfering objects. Furthermore, the discrete patches of segmented visual input align more seamlessly with how MLLMs structure visual tokens via positional embeddings. In experiments, we evaluate SegAnswer across diverse benchmarks, including high-resolution perception, general perception, and hallucination. It achieves consistent improvements and also exhibits considerable performance on segmentation tasks, validating its capability for reliable pixel grounding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: pixel-level segmentation for MLLM visual reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 像素级分割用于多模态大语言模型视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 像素级分割用于多模态大语言模型视觉推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 像素级分割用于多模态大语言模型视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SegAnswer replaces bounding boxes with pixel-level segmentation masks for region-of-interest isolation in MLLMs, improving visual reasoning accuracy by filtering background noise.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 面向多模态大语言模型的视觉推理，SegAnswer提出将缩放操作单元从边界框切换到像素级分割掩码。通过细粒度掩码隔离目标区域，能更精确地过滤杂乱背景和干扰物体。在多个视觉推理基准上，该方法显著提升了推理准确性，展现了细粒度视觉定位对推理质量的促进作用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 面向多模态大语言模型的视觉推理，SegAnswer提出将缩放操作单元从边界框切换到像素级分割掩码。通过细粒度掩码隔离目标区域，能更精确地过滤杂乱背景和干扰物体。在多个视觉推理基准上，该方法显著提升了推理准确性，展现了细粒度视觉定位对推理质量的促进作用。

quick_skim.title: SearchEyes: Towards Frontier Multimodal Deep Search Intelligence via Search World Simulation
quick_skim.abstract: Training multimodal search agents to perform multi-hop reasoning remains challenging due to a fundamental structural disconnect: existing pipelines construct training data, search environments, and reward signals independently, causing synthesized structural metadata to be discarded, environments to rely on irreproducible external engines, and RL rewards to remain sparse at the trajectory level. We present \textbf{SearchEyes}, which uses a typed knowledge graph as the backbone of a \emph{simulated search world} that unifies all three components. We propose \textbf{Perception-Knowledge Chains (PKC)} to sample constrained multi-hop paths over the visual-knowledge intersection of Wikidata5M, retaining hop-level entity metadata that simultaneously defines a self-contained search world and step-level reward anchors. We further propose \textbf{Hop-Anchored Policy Optimization (HaPO)}, which reuses these anchors for step-level credit assignment without a separately trained process reward model. Experiments on six multimodal knowledge-intensive benchmarks show that SearchEyes achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source multimodal search agents, with SearchEyes-27B improving over the strongest open-source baseline by 6.2 points on average.%
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multimodal deep search with multi-hop reasoning via perception-knowledge chains in simulated search world
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过感知-知识链在模拟搜索世界中进行多模态深度搜索和多跳推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过感知-知识链在模拟搜索世界中进行多模态深度搜索和多跳推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过感知-知识链在模拟搜索世界中进行多模态深度搜索和多跳推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SearchEyes trains multimodal search agents for multi-hop reasoning using a simulated search world with typed knowledge graph and perception-knowledge chains.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态搜索智能体的训练面临数据、环境和奖励信号脱节的问题。本文提出SearchEyes，以类型化知识图为骨干构建模拟搜索世界，统一三者。并设计感知-知识链（PKC）在视觉-知识交集上采样受约束的多跳路径，保留层级实体元数据，用于强化学习训练。实验表明，该方法显著提升了多跳搜索推理能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态搜索智能体的训练面临数据、环境和奖励信号脱节的问题。本文提出SearchEyes，以类型化知识图为骨干构建模拟搜索世界，统一三者。并设计感知-知识链（PKC）在视觉-知识交集上采样受约束的多跳路径，保留层级实体元数据，用于强化学习训练。实验表明，该方法显著提升了多跳搜索推理能力。

quick_skim.title: HoloCount: A Holistic Visual Counting Benchmark for MLLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Visual counting is a fundamental pillar of multimodal intelligence, requiring a seamless integration of fine-grained grounding and spatial reasoning. While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in qualitative scene understanding, their quantitative precision remains a significant bottleneck, often characterized by persistent numerical hallucinations. Existing counting benchmarks primarily focus on basic perception in simplified contexts, failing to capture the complex failure modes that emerge under logical constraints or adversarial conditions. To address these limitations, we introduce HoloCount, a holistic and diagnostically rich benchmark structured around a three-level hierarchical taxonomy. HoloCount evaluates MLLMs across: (1) Semantic Counting, focusing on atomic and property-based enumeration; (2) Analytical Counting, assessing logical composition through spatial and set-based reasoning; and (3) Robustness Testing, probing model integrity against adverse scenarios and grounded counter-priors, such as high-density scenes and linguistic biases. Through an exhaustive evaluation of over 20 state-of-the-art MLLMs, we reveal a critical performance gap: even top-tier models degrade significantly as tasks transition from perception to complex analytical reasoning and adverse scenarios. Our findings provide a systematic landscape of current MLLM counting capabilities and offer a roadmap for developing more grounded and reliable multimodal systems. The dataset is available at https://mm-mvr.github.io/HoloCount/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: holistic visual counting benchmark revealing numerical hallucinations in MLLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 揭示多模态大语言模型数字幻觉的全面视觉计数基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 揭示多模态大语言模型数字幻觉的全面视觉计数基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 揭示多模态大语言模型数字幻觉的全面视觉计数基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces HoloCount, a holistic benchmark for visual counting in MLLMs, exposing persistent numerical hallucinations under logical constraints and adversarial conditions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态大语言模型在视觉计数上存在严重的数字幻觉，现有基准过于简单。本文提出HoloCount，一个基于三级层次分类法的全面诊断基准，评估模型在逻辑约束和对抗条件下的计数能力。实验揭示了MLLM在定量精确性上的系统性失败，为缓解数值幻觉提供了重要测试平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多模态大语言模型在视觉计数上存在严重的数字幻觉，现有基准过于简单。本文提出HoloCount，一个基于三级层次分类法的全面诊断基准，评估模型在逻辑约束和对抗条件下的计数能力。实验揭示了MLLM在定量精确性上的系统性失败，为缓解数值幻觉提供了重要测试平台。
